title: Garlicky, Snappy, Monsoon Jamaican Fish Bowl
description: Crisp fried white fish, fragrant tadka-spiced rice, and a tangy slaw, all served in a sturdy bento box. Perfect for a quick and refreshing lunch on a stormy day.
instructions: Heat the coconut oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the onion and cook until softened, about 3 minutes.
Add the ginger and garlic and cook for another minute, stirring constantly.
Add the flaxseed meal and tadka spice blend and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the brown rice and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly.
Transfer the rice mixture to a separate pot and add 2 cups of water. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer, covered, for 15-20 minutes.
While the rice is cooking, prepare the slaw by combining the carrots, red cabbage, apple cider vinegar, and lime juice in a bowl.
Season the fish fillets with salt and black pepper.
Heat a separate skillet over medium-high heat and add a small amount of oil. Cook the fish fillets for 3-4 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
To assemble the bowls, place a portion of the cooked rice in the bottom of a bento box, followed by a cooked fish fillet, and then a spoonful of the slaw.
